description: Fix the Module Federation RUNTIME-010 crash on /store?cat=laptops. The host MF runtime is already initialised at build time by @nx/module-federation with name 'host'; MicroFrontendApp.tsx then calls init() again with name '@ecommerce-host', which the MF runtime rejects with 'The name option cannot be changed after initialization' (#RUNTIME-010). The fix is to replace init() with registerRemotes() and guard each registration with a module-level Set so each remote registers at most once. Done when: navigating to /store (with any query string) renders the store micro-frontend without error; no RUNTIME-010 in the browser console; re-navigating to /store does not produce 'remote already registered' warnings.

userStories.title: Replace init() with registerRemotes() in MicroFrontendApp.tsx
acceptanceCriteria: A module-level `const registeredRemotes = new Set<string>()` guards duplicate registrations
The init() call is replaced by a registerRemotes() call guarded by the Set
No other file in micro-frontends/host is modified
userStories.notes: Exact file: micro-frontends/host/src/microFe/MicroFrontendApp.tsx. Buggy lines: 17 (import) and 93-101 (init call). The module-federation.config.ts for host (name: 'host') and all remotes must NOT be changed — only MicroFrontendApp.tsx is modified.

userStories.title: Browser-verify store loads without RUNTIME-010 error
userStories.description: As a QA engineer, I need to confirm that http://localhost:4200/store?cat=laptops renders the store micro-frontend without the 'Failed to Load Micro Frontend' error boundary, and that re-navigating to /store does not emit 'remote already registered' warnings in the console.
acceptanceCriteria: Navigate to http://localhost:4200/store?cat=laptops — the store product list renders (no ErrorBoundary shown)
Browser DevTools console contains no RUNTIME-010 error
Navigate away and back to /store — no 'remote already registered' warning in console
Screenshot attached to progress.txt confirming the rendered store page
userStories.notes: Depends on US-001. Requires the dev servers to be running: `cd micro-frontends && npm start` (serves host on :4200, store on :4201). Use a headless browser or Playwright to capture the screenshot.
